legal_framework.naloxone_legal_status: LEGAL — Naloxone is legal in Pennsylvania and available statewide under a Department of Health naloxone standing order that serves as a prescription for the general public and community organizations.

good_samaritan_overdose_immunity.scope: Limited immunity from charge and prosecution for personal-use drug possession, paraphernalia, and some probation/parole violations when someone in good faith reports an overdose, reasonably believes an overdose is occurring, stays with the victim, and cooperates with responders; the victim’s immunity is contingent on the caller qualifying.

pharmacies.mechanism: Statewide standing order and OTC — many pharmacies can dispense prescription naloxone under the statewide standing order or sell FDA-approved OTC naloxone, and the standing order can be used to obtain insurance coverage for OTC products.
pharmacies.medicaid_coverage_notes: Covered — Pennsylvania Medical Assistance fee‑for‑service and managed care plans will pay for naloxone for beneficiaries when billed under the standing order, with no prior authorization and no limit on the number of times naloxone can be purchased through Medical Assistance.
pharmacies.typical_cost: ~$40–50 USD cash price per two‑dose box of naloxone nasal spray at retail pharmacies (e.g., GoodRx shows around 44 USD discounted price versus a list price in the high 60s for a box of two 4 mg sprays).

practical_guidance.how_to_get_naloxone_quickly: Walk into a participating pharmacy and use the statewide standing order or buy OTC; request a free kit via the statewide Mail‑to‑Home Naloxone Program operated with Prevention Point Pittsburgh and NEXT Distro; or obtain no‑cost naloxone from local Overdose Prevention Program and other community partners.
practical_guidance.barriers_and_workarounds: Immunity rules are conditional and can be confusing (contingent on caller behavior and covering only some offenses), not all pharmacies stock or actively promote naloxone, and people may face stigma or uncertainty about when they are legally protected.
Excellent access state.
First caller limit; community dist. advised.
Lack of immunity; mail order preferred.
Rural access; use NEXT Distro.
Weak immunity; cost barriers.

legal_framework.naloxone_legal_status: Legal — Naloxone and other opioid antagonists may be prescribed, dispensed, possessed, and administered under state law and a statewide standing order.

good_samaritan_overdose_immunity.scope: Limited — Affirmative defense for certain possession/paraphernalia charges when someone reports an overdose, stays, and cooperates; not blanket immunity from arrest or all charges.

pharmacies.mechanism: Statewide standing order plus OTC — Pharmacists can dispense under the standing order, and Narcan and similar products are also sold OTC.
pharmacies.medicaid_coverage_notes: Covered — All forms of opioid antagonists under the standing order are covered by Medicaid without prior authorization for Medicaid patients of record.

practical_guidance.how_to_get_naloxone_quickly: Pharmacy (standing order or OTC) or free kits from sites listed on Stop the Opidemic and UtahNaloxone.org (health departments, libraries, community partners)
practical_guidance.barriers_and_workarounds: Rural pharmacy access; out‑of‑pocket cost for uninsured people; variable pharmacy stocking; confusion about legal protections and OTC vs prescription routes.
Strong laws aid access.
Rural SW VA has dedicated programs.
State mail program is robust.
